opp.ais_why:
• Reviewed 4 calls spanning 4/1/25-1/21/26. Zero AI signals detected across all transcripts.
• Calls focused on procurement process modernization, ERP upgrades, decentralized purchasing challenges, but no AI mentioned.
• Recommend probing: "What's your AI strategy for procurement?" and "Interest in AI-powered contract analysis or autonomous sourcing?"

No quantified metrics in fields or calls

sources: No Avoma transcripts discovered this sweep
metrics.narrative: No quantified value case is on record. The pain themes are qualitative: procurement process modernization, an S/4HANA ERP migration, and decentralized purchasing (per the AIS review of prior calls and the 2026-06-25 follow-up email citing 'the context around the S/4HANA timeline'). No cost, cycle-time, spend-under-management, or invoice-error figures have been captured, and there are no transcribed Avoma calls this sweep to extract them from. Quantifying the procurement baseline with Artana and the incoming finance director before the RFP locks is the highest-leverage metrics move.

champion.narrative: Artana Skreli-Fitzgerald (Global Procurement Manager, Evaluator) is a developing and genuinely active champion: she suggested Zycus connect with Joe on procurement initiatives (2026-06-29), is bringing her finance director into the pre-RFP call (2026-06-22), and is coordinating the NDA and RFP cadence. What is still missing is proof she has direct economic-buyer access and will defend Zycus inside a competitive RFP. Deepening her sponsorship and confirming her reach to the budget owner is the next champion step.

sources: No Avoma/tasks/Next Step naming a competitor
competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source: Competitors__c is blank and no call, email, task, or Next Step names a rival. A formal S2P RFP is imminent (2026-06-22), so a competitive field is highly likely to open, and the S/4HANA migration makes the SAP-adjacent stack a natural consideration, but nothing in the evidence names SAP Ariba or any other vendor, so none is asserted. Surfacing the vendor shortlist during pre-RFP discovery, before criteria harden, is the priority.

sources: AIS_Why__c (procurement modernization, ERP upgrades, decentralized purchasing)
identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed pain centres on procurement modernization tied to an S/4HANA ERP migration and decentralized purchasing (AIS call review; 2026-06-25 email on the S/4HANA timeline). The pain owner is procurement, championed by Artana Skreli-Fitzgerald, with finance about to weigh in. The compelling event is the S/4HANA program: aligning the S2P selection to that timeline is the wedge. The pain is real but not yet quantified or tied to a dollar impact.

sources: Zycus NDA response 2026-07-02 (okay to execute, signer Alexa Bradley)
paper_process.narrative: Paper mechanics are actively moving early, which is a strong signal for an early-stage deal. The buyer sent an NDA on 2026-07-01 asking Zycus to 'review, redline if appropriate, complete the required information and return'; Zycus reviewed it 'okay to execute' with signer Alexa Bradley (VP Sales) on 2026-07-02, and the emails reference 'working on Legal cycles.' MSA/SOW, InfoSec, and the S/4HANA integration scope are not yet defined and become relevant once the RFP lands.

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority is approaching but not yet named. On 2026-06-22 the buyer wrote 'I will be bringing our finance director in the call,' signalling finance will enter the evaluation, but that person is not in any Salesforce contact role, so they cannot be named or titled yet. Artana Skreli-Fitzgerald (Global Procurement Manager, Evaluator) drives procurement but does not control a $250k multi-year budget alone. Converting the incoming finance director into a mapped, named budget owner is the top EB action.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured path: a pre-RFP alignment call (2026-06-22 request, met 2026-06-25), a formal S2P RFP to follow, and a legal/NDA cycle already open (NDA received 2026-07-01, cleared to execute 2026-07-02). Finance is being pulled in, indicating a multi-function committee (procurement plus finance). The full approval chain, RFP timeline, and target decision date are still unstated and should be captured in writing.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are forming around a formal S2P RFP the buyer is 'gearing up to send' (2026-06-22) and an S/4HANA migration that will make ERP integration a scored requirement. The product scope (eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin) frames the functional fit, but the buyer has not yet shared written evaluation criteria or scoring. Securing the RFP requirements document early lets Zycus shape criteria before they harden.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.win_position: Win 30 is carried by momentum 72 vs stage-expected 50; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 72 (moving forward): seniority rising (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 24 is anchored on the stage win (30), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 72, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

deal_movement.summary: After months of low-priority dormancy, the deal re-ignited in late June 2026 under a new owner: a pre-RFP buyer meeting, a live NDA/legal cycle, and a buyer-announced S2P RFP with finance joining. The Dec 8 'paused to mid-January' Next Step is stale and superseded.

critical_signals.text: The 2026-06-25 alignment call surfaced the S/4HANA migration timeline and confirmed a formal S2P RFP is coming, restarting real momentum.
The NDA was reviewed and cleared to execute on 2026-07-02, opening the legal cycle unusually early for a qualified deal.
The buyer is bringing their finance director into the pre-RFP call, so budget authority is approaching but Zycus has not met them yet.
The buyer is preparing a formal S2P RFP that will define scope and scoring, so shaping those criteria now is decisive.
No rival is named yet, but the forming RFP will open a competitive field, so the vendor shortlist must be surfaced early.

champion_strength.summary: Artana Skreli-Fitzgerald is a developing but genuinely active champion: she re-engaged Zycus, is coordinating the NDA and RFP, and is bringing her finance director in. Still unproven: direct economic-buyer access and willingness to defend Zycus in a competitive RFP.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is warming sharply: buyer-initiated re-engagement, an NDA cleared to execute, and a forming RFP with finance joining. Not cooling.

north_star_verdict.math: 210 days to a 2027-01-29 close; created 2025-04-01, moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 2026-06-29 (qualified 2026-06-30). The stale 'paused to mid-January' Next Step is superseded by heavy fresh activity: a 2026-06-25 buyer meeting on the S/4HANA timeline and upcoming RFP, an NDA reviewed 'okay to execute' 2026-07-02, and a 2026-07-06 meeting. Last activity 2026-07-06. For an early-stage deal with a buyer-driven RFP forming and legal cycles opening, the long runway to Jan 2027 is comfortably credible.
evidence: Buyer email 2026-06-22: 'We are gearing up to sending out a S2P RFP and I am looking to schedule a quick call with you beforehand. I will be bringing our finance director in the call.'

Last activity 2026-07-06; deal is live, not paused

The single most important competitive action is to surface the buyer's vendor shortlist during pre-RFP discovery before the criteria are locked.
